nuggets.headline: Daft Punk Crafted 'Homework' Working Just 8 Hours a Week
nuggets.text: Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo assembled the 16 tracks for _Homework_ over five months, working casually in Bangalter's bedroom. Their work ethic was deliberately relaxed—no more than eight hours a week—as they initially had no intention of making a full album, focusing instead on crafting standalone singles.

source.title: Daft Punk: Homework Album Review | Pitchfork
source.quoteSnippet: They casually worked on the 16 tunes for Homework in Thomas Bangalter's bedroom, and their work ethic while assembling it was no more than eight hours a week, over five months.

nuggets.imageCaption: Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo, circa the Homework era.

nuggets.headline: 'Around the World' Got Its Final Mix on an 11-Year-Old's Boombox
nuggets.text: After tracking the song's elements to DAT tape and looping them in samplers like the E-mu SP-1200, the final mixdown happened on a JVC boombox Thomas Bangalter had owned since he was 11. The duo credited this unconventional method for giving the track its distinctive lo-fi warmth, a character that defined the sound of the entire _Homework_ album. Listen for that slightly compressed, unified texture that glues the bass, drums, and synth together.

source.title: Around the World (Daft Punk song)
source.quoteSnippet: Final mixing occurred on an old JVC boombox that Bangalter had owned since age 11. The duo credited this unconventional mixing method for imparting a distinctive lo-fi warmth to both Homework and their follow-up Discovery.

nuggets.imageCaption: Around the World by Daft Punk

nuggets.headline: For Another Take on French Retro-Futurism, Explore Kavinsky
nuggets.text: While "Around the World" recontextualizes '70s funk, fellow French producer Kavinsky applies a similar retro-futurist lens to '80s synth-noir and film scores. His work shares Daft Punk's affinity for analog gear and strong narrative concepts, creating cinematic electronic music that feels like a direct descendant of the scene _Homework_ helped build.

source.title: DAFT PUNK
source.quoteSnippet: Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo describes their sound as 'A mix between the past and the future, maybe the present.'

nuggets.imageCaption: French electronic artist Kavinsky, known for his '80s-inspired synthwave sound.

artistSummary: Daft Punk, the Parisian duo of Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo, revolutionized dance music with their enigmatic robot personas and a sound that fused analog warmth with digital precision. They twisted the past of funk and disco into a futuristic vision, ascending to pop iconography. Their work is a dialogue between the human and the machine, celebrating both soulful imperfection and synthetic perfection.
